title: Avient Announces Quarterly Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Avient Corporation's Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.275 per share, payable on July 15, 2026, to shareholders of record on June 18, 2026. The company continues to position itself as an innovator in materials solutions with a global workforce of 9,000 employees.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a quarterly dividend demonstrates financial stability and confidence in the company's cash flow generation. Regular dividend payments are typically viewed positively by investors as they indicate profitability and shareholder-friendly capital allocation policies.

title: Avient Announces Quarterly Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Avient Corporation's Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.275 per share, payable on April 3, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 17, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's financial health and confidence in its cash flow generation. Regular dividend payments are generally viewed positively by investors as they indicate stable earnings and management's commitment to returning value to shareholders.

title: Avient Announces Fifteenth Consecutive Annual Dividend Increase
author: Prnewswire
description: Avient Corporation has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.275 per share, marking its fifteenth consecutive annual dividend increase. The dividend will be paid on January 7, 2026, to stockholders of record on December 12, 2025.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrated consistent financial performance by increasing its dividend for the fifteenth consecutive year, indicating stable earnings growth and commitment to shareholder value

title: Earnings Summary on Avient
author: Jesterai
description: Avient reported Q2 2025 earnings of $0.80 per share, exceeding analyst expectations. The company saw modest revenue growth of 2% to $866.5 million, with strength in healthcare and defense markets offsetting weakness in consumer and transportation sectors.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed performance with revenue growth and earnings beat, but facing challenges in consumer and transportation markets. Positive aspects include resilience in healthcare and defense segments, continued cost management, and debt reduction efforts.

title: Thermoplastic Elastomers Benchmark Report 2025: Arkema, Avient, BASF, Celanese, Covestro, Dow, Eastman, Envalior, Evonik, ExxonMobil, Huntsman, Kraton, Kuraray, LCY, Lubrizol LyondellBasell, Mitsui
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The report analyzes 17 leading thermoplastic elastomer (TPE) providers, including TPU and TPV, highlighting their strengths and growth potential. TPEs are versatile materials used in various industries, and the report assesses their performance across 10 growth and innovation criteria.
